key_insight: A coupled LSTM plus physics-informed GNN can reconstruct nonlinear, history-dependent local stress fields by separating temporal path dependence from spatial field inference.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate stress prediction for heterogeneous microstructures, cutting finite-element runtime by orders of magnitude while retaining accuracy across different mesh resolutions and element types.
method: LSTM encodes the macroscopic stress-strain loading history into a compact hidden state.
Physics-informed GNN/MeshGraphNet reconstructs the spatial stress field on the microstructure graph at each time step.
A warm-up weighting scheme balances reconstruction loss with a discrete divergence-based equilibrium penalty.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a periodic plate-with-a-hole microstructure, the model achieves about 1000× speedup over FE and generalizes to sequences twice as long with 1.9% cumulative error.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of mesh connectivity and physics loss balancing, so training stability and transfer to new material laws or markedly different geometries may require re-tuning.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
